name: The Andes Hotel
description: A beloved Catskills institution — casual dining, craft cocktails, and live music in a beautifully restored 19th-century hotel right in the village of Andes.
seasonal: Hours may vary seasonally — call ahead in winter.

name: Brushland Eating House
description: Farm-to-table restaurant a converted farmhouse in Bovina Center. Seasonal menus sourced directly from local Catskill farms.
seasonal: Schedule changes seasonally — always check website before visiting.

name: The Bull & Garland
description: An authentic British pub tucked away in the charming village of Hobart. Classic pub food, real ales on draught, and a wonderfully genuine neighborhood atmosphere.

name: Peekamoose Restaurant
description: Chef Devin Mills' highly regarded fine dining destination in Big Indian. Elegant seasonal cuisine, an outstanding wine program, and a romantic setting.
seasonal: Closed Tuesdays & Wednesdays. Reservations strongly recommended.

name: Pakatakan Farmers Market
description: One of the Catskills' best farmers markets, held in the stunning octagonal Halcottsville barn. Fresh produce, local dairy, baked goods, meat, flowers, and crafts.

name: Catskill Distilling Co.
description: Award-winning craft distillery producing whiskey, gin, and vodka from local grains. Tours, tastings, and a beautiful tasting room in Bethel.

name: Pepacton Reservoir
description: One of NYC's largest reservoirs — a stunning expanse of still water surrounded by forested hills. Exceptional birdwatching, scenic drives, and fishing (NYS fishing license + permit required).
seasonal: Fall foliage is spectacular mid-October. Winter views are dramatic.

name: Belleayre Mountain
description: State-owned ski resort — 50+ trails, terrain parks, and a gondola. Also offers hiking, mountain biking, and summer concerts. Great for all skill levels.
seasonal: Night skiing on select dates; check website for conditions.

name: Hunter Mountain
description: One of the largest ski resorts in the Catskills with 67 trails and extensive snowmaking. About 30 miles east of Andes. Strong intermediate and expert terrain.
seasonal: 100% snowmaking coverage keeps the mountain open even in warm winters.

name: Windham Mountain
description: Family-friendly resort with 54 trails and a relaxed Catskills vibe. About 35 miles east. Known for excellent grooming and a welcoming atmosphere for beginners.
seasonal: First Tracks program offers early morning groomed runs before lifts open to the public.

name: Plattekill Mountain
description: Ski resort in the Catskills.

name: Balsam Lake Mtn. Trail
description: A Catskill 3500 peak with a restored fire tower offering sweeping 360° views. A rewarding moderate hike — roughly 5.5 miles round trip with 1,000 ft. elevation gain.
seasonal: Trail may be icy or snowy December–March. Microspikes recommended.

name: Catskill Center Trails
description: The Catskill Center stewards dozens of miles of trail across their preserve properties. Stop by or visit their website for trail maps, guided hikes, and conservation programs.

name: Delhi
description: The Delaware County seat with diners, shops, supermarket and SUNY Delaware campus

name: Margaretville
description: Charming village with restaurants and shops along the Delaware

name: Andes
description: Artsy and charming village with local shops and restaurants

name: Little Grocer
description: Local grocery and provisions.

name: Andes General Store
description: NEW Local grocery and provisions.

name: Byebrook Farm
description: Farmstand with local produce and goods.

name: Burn Ayr Farm
description: Maple and farm products.

name: Brookside Farm
description: Maple and farm products.
